item.title: Diamondback Sees 10% More Permian Oil Rigs by Year-End as War Boosts Prices
item.description: Diamondback Energy Inc., one of the biggest US shale producers, predicts that drillers will add as many as 30 rigs to America’s most prolific oil field by the end of the year as the Iran war sends crude prices soaring.

item.title: Ambev Soars Most Since 1999 as Brazil Beer Boosts Earnings
item.description: Shares of Ambev SA, the Brazilian unit of Anheuser-Busch InBev NV, soared the most in nearly 27 years after stronger-than-expected beer volumes in the South American nation drove a beat in first quarter earnings.

item.title: Anthropic Unveils AI Agents to Field Financial Services Tasks
item.description: Anthropic PBC unveiled a set of new artificial intelligence agents designed to handle a broader mix of financial services tasks, part of the company’s push to win over Wall Street.

item.title: Inflationary Signals Are Popping up Everywhere
item.description: It’s not just the US.

item.title: FX Risk Keeping Opportunistic Bond Buyers Local, BNY Says
item.description: Soaring government bond yields are attracting dip-buyers eager to lock in lofty, long-term returns — but only within their own borders, according to BNY.

item.title: Wall Street Is Trusting Bots With Biggest Corporate Bond Trades
item.description: Wall Street securities dealers and money managers are increasingly relying on algorithms to execute even the biggest corporate-bond trades, the latest sign of movement toward electronic trading in a $12 trillion market that was long one of the biggest holdouts.

item.title: Brazilian AI Legal Startup Enter Valued at $1.2 Billion in Round
item.description: Leia em português.

item.title: Iran Oil Tanker Likely Beat US Blockade, Satellite Tracker Says
item.description: One Iranian oil tanker may have slipped through a US blockade of the Persian Gulf state’s shipping, according to TankerTrackers.com, a firm that uses satellite imagery to monitor vessel movements.

item.title: Fed’s Bowman Says Consumer Fraud Poses Risks to Financial System
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop said that consumer fraud and scams pose increasing risks to the financial system, emphasizing that “nearly every fraud” affects a bank account or is tied to a payment that involves a bank account.

item.title: Kganyago Links ‘Surprisingly Resilient’ Rand to Dollar Mistrust
item.description: South African Reserve Bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago heralded the fortitude of the rand and other emerging-market currencies amid the Iran war, and said this could reflect a wider souring on US assets.

item.title: US Job Openings Fell Slightly in March as Hiring Rebounded
item.description: US job openings were little changed in March and hiring rebounded as the labor market showed continued signs of stabilization.

item.title: BNP Sees Two South African Rate Hikes in Hawkish Inflation Fight
item.description: The South African Reserve Bank will likely raise interest rates at its next two meetings to rein in inflation that’s set to surge because of energy supply-side shocks caused by the war in Iran, BNP Paribas said.

item.title: Hormuz Remains Largely Deserted as US and Iran Vie for Control
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely empty, with no new commercial ship crossings recorded despite a US effort to guide vessels through the waterway.

item.title: US New-Home Sales Increased in March to Fastest Pace of Year
item.description: US new-home sales rose in March by more than forecast as the median selling price slid to a more than four-year low and builders offered incentives.

item.title: Zimbabwe Starts Trials to Blend Ethanol and Diesel to Cut Costs
item.description: Zimbabwe began trials of blending ethanol and diesel to offset rising costs caused by conflict in the Middle East, the nation’s energy minister said.

item.title: Portugal to Propose Energy Windfall Tax as War Raises Bills
item.description: Portugal plans to push ahead with a proposal to impose a windfall tax on energy companies to help cushion the fallout from spiraling costs for consumers and companies caused by the Iran war.

item.title: SAP Plans to Expand AI Access to Customers Who Don’t Use Cloud
item.description: SAP SE is planning to expand access to its artificial intelligence solutions to some customers who haven’t moved their systems to its cloud services, people familiar with the matter said, a strategic shift prioritizing the newer technology as the race to keep customers away from rivals heats up.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Posts Biggest Deficit Since 2018 On Hormuz Closure
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s fiscal deficit widened in the first quarter to the highest level since 2018 as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz forced it to reduce oil exports and spending on projects to diversify the economy continued to rise.

item.title: France Unveils Rules to Reduce Reliance on China for Rare Earths
item.description: France announced fresh measures to curb reliance on Chinese rare earths in a move to safeguard industries ranging from consumer electronics to electric cars and offshore wind.

item.title: Unemployment for Recent College Grads Remains High, NY Fed Says
item.description: The unemployment rate for recent college graduates in the US remained high in the first three months of 2026, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

item.title: Apollo’s Slok Likens AI Impact to Best Aspects of ‘China Shock’
item.description: Productivity gains from artificial intelligence will probably result in more jobs being created than are lost, similar to what occurred after China joined the World Trade Organization, according to Torsten Slok of Apollo Global Management Inc.

item.title: Ecuador Looks to Tap Global Debt Markets Again Amid Oil Jump
item.description: Ecuador is looking to tap international bond markets for the second time this year, seizing on its status as an oil exporter and joining a borrowing spree in emerging markets.

item.title: Jamie Foxx, Squid Game Creator Invest in AI Startup ElevenLabs
item.description: Artificial intelligence developer ElevenLabs has brought on a wave of new investors including BlackRock Inc., Nvidia Corp., actors Jamie Foxx and Eva Longoria, and Hwang Dong-hyuk, the creator of the hit series Squid Game.

item.title: Former Trump NASA Head Bridenstine to Lead Startup Quantum Space
item.description: Former NASA administrator Jim Bridenstine is joining Quantum Space, a startup developing maneuverable spacecraft for defense missions, as chief executive officer, the company said on Tuesday.

item.title: Ford’s $30,000 Electric Pickup Aims to Win Over Skeptical Buyers
item.description: Everything about Ford Motor Co.’s Long Beach, California, “skunkworks” operation was designed so that its crack engineering team could dream up a new electric vehicle lineup that broke with convention. And yet after years of tinkering, the automaker is aiming to reinvent a form it didn’t get right the first time: the pickup truck.

item.title: Brazil Central Bank Says It’s Alert to Greater Price Pressures
item.description: Brazil’s central bank said its monetary policy needs to remain tight as both the war in the Middle East and domestic demand pressure inflation.

item.title: US Trade Gap Widened in March as Imports Outpaced Exports
item.description: The US trade deficit widened in March as an increase in the value of imports exceeded exports in a sign of solid consumer and business demand.

item.title: Updates From Milken Institute Global Conference
item.description: Speakers participate in a panel discussion during the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills, California. Photographer: Lauren Justice/Bloomberg

item.title: Canada Swings to Trade Surplus as Oil, Gold Exports Jump
item.description: Canada posted its first trade surplus in six months due to surging exports of gold and oil in March as the Iran war drove up global uncertainty.

item.title: ECB’s Lagarde Urges Europe to Cut Reliance on Energy Imports
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said soaring energy costs due to the Iran war should act as a wake-up call for Europe to reduce its reliance on fossil fuels and energy imports.
